mechanism_narrative: An always-on positive retail-sales trend signal states that stronger 3-month growth in RSAFS raises expected corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:latest snapshot RSAFS=733258.0 as of t-3mo
failure_condition: Reject if the walk-forward Rank IC between 3-month RSAFS growth and 20-day S&P 500 returns is below 0.03 over two consecutive 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A downward crossing in FEDFUNDS signals monetary easing and lower equity discount rates; when FEDFUNDS falls below its 6-month moving average, S&P 500 returns should be positive over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses below its 6-month moving average
evidence_refs: fact:latest snapshot FEDFUNDS=3.88 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns no more often than the unconditional base rate, or if mean triggered returns are not higher than baseline at p <= 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in UNRATE signals weakening labor income and rising recession risk; when UNRATE increases by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months, S&P 500 returns should be negative over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:latest snapshot UNRATE=4.4 as of t-3mo
failure_condition: Reject if more than 55% of triggered cases have positive 60-day S&P 500 returns, or if triggered mean returns exceed unconditional mean returns over a 10-year walk-forward sample.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO is expanding while CPIAUCSL inflation momentum is decelerating, the macro mix signals real output growth without renewed margin pressure, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month change > 0 and CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change below its 6-month annualized change
evidence_refs: fact:latest snapshot INDPRO=101.4279 and CPIAUCSL=324.368 as of t-3mo
failure_condition: Reject if the joint signal does not outperform either INDPRO-only or CPIAUCSL-only signals in 20-day return hit rate by at least 3 percentage points in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in HOUST signals weakening interest-rate-sensitive demand and future pressure on cyclical earnings; when HOUST falls more than 10% over 3 months, S&P 500 returns should be negative over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:latest snapshot HOUST=1307.0 as of t-4mo
failure_condition: Reject if triggered 60-day S&P 500 returns are positive in more than 60% of cases or if average triggered returns are not below unconditional returns at p <= 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS remains high and CPIAUCSL inflation momentum accelerates, equities face simultaneous discount-rate pressure and margin compression, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 3.5 and CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change above its 6-month annualized change
failure_condition: Reject if the interaction term FEDFUNDS high x CPIAUCSL acceleration has a nonnegative coefficient for 5-day returns in walk-forward logistic regressions across two consecutive 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Positive PAYEMS growth combined with stable or falling UNRATE signals durable household income and low near-term recession risk, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:latest snapshot PAYEMS=159626 and UNRATE=4.4 as of t-3mo
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS and UNRATE signal has lower 20-day hit rate than the unconditional base rate, or if its incremental AUC versus single-series models is less than 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: An always-on real-activity trend signal states that stronger GDPC1 growth raises expected aggregate earnings and risk appetite, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:latest snapshot GDPC1=23770.976 as of t-8mo
failure_condition: Reject if real-time GDPC1 growth has Rank IC below 0.03 with 60-day S&P 500 returns over any 5-year rolling window after accounting for publication lags.
